title: Global Markets Brief — 28 May 2026
intro: Risk-off sentiment prevails as Asian markets digest overnight weakness, with USD strength broadening across majors. Oil's sharp rally on Middle East supply concerns contrasts with precious metals selling pressure as real yields tick higher. Equity markets show mixed sentiment with US indices resilient while European and Asian bourses face headwinds.

sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk-off sentiment prevails as Asian markets digest overnight weakness, with USD strength broadening across majors. Oil's sharp rally on Middle East supply concerns contrasts with precious metals selling pressure as real yields tick higher. Equity markets show mixed sentiment with US indices resilient while European and Asian bourses face headwinds.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
Strongest to Weakest: USD (+0.25%), CHF, CAD, JPY, EUR (-0.13%), GBP (-0.19%), AUD (-0.22%), NZD (-0.22%)
USD dominance continues with broad-based strength against commodity currencies and risk-sensitive pairs. Safe-haven flows favor USD and CHF as geopolitical tensions support defensive positioning.

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1614: Bearish momentum persists below 1.1650 resistance. ECB dovish tilt weighs on single currency with 1.1580 support zone under pressure. Target 1.1550 on breakdown.
USD/JPY 159.5040: Testing multi-decade highs with intervention risks elevated above 159.50. BoJ verbal warnings intensify but market remains skeptical of concrete action. Resistance at 160.00 psychological level.
GBP/USD 1.3402: Cable weakness accelerates below 1.3450 support. BoE pause expectations and UK fiscal concerns drive selling. Next support cluster at 1.3350-1.3380.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• USD long positioning extends with systematic buying across majors
• Commodity currency weakness reflects China growth concerns and defensive rotation
• JPY intervention hedges increase as USD/JPY approaches critical thresholds
• EM outflows accelerate on Fed hawkish repricing and risk-off sentiment

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content:
Performance Leaders: Dow Jones +0.36%, Nasdaq +0.07%, S&P 500 -0.02%
Laggards: FTSE 100 -0.90%, Nikkei 225 -0.47%, CAC 40 -0.32%, DAX -0.14%
US markets show resilience with value outperforming growth. European indices weaken on energy sector pressure and macro uncertainty. Asian session reflects cautious sentiment ahead of key data releases.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold 4423.50: Sharp -1.29% decline as real yields rise. Key support at 4400 psychological level with bearish momentum targeting 4350. Fed policy uncertainty weighs on yellow metal.
WTI Crude 91.24: Impressive +2.89% surge on Middle East supply disruption fears. Break above 90 resistance opens path to 95 target. Geopolitical premium sustains bullish bias.
Silver 73.67: Industrial metal weakness with -1.64% drop. Following gold's bearish lead with 72.00 support zone critical for bulls.
Natural Gas 3.072: Mild -0.74% decline as weather patterns normalize. Range-bound between 3.00-3.20 support/resistance.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC 73291: Down -1.40% in risk-off environment. Critical support at 72000 with resistance at 75000. Institutional flows remain subdued amid macro uncertainty.
ETH 1991.76: Underperforming with -1.49% decline. ETH/BTC ratio weakening suggests altcoin rotation pressure. Support at 1950 level monitored closely.
Top Movers: XRP 1.2907 (-1.18%) shows relative resilience, while SOL 81.12 (-1.51%) and DOGE 0.0986 (-1.82%) face selling pressure.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• WTI Crude: Geopolitical premium supports rally continuation
• USD/CHF: Safe-haven USD strength targets 0.79
• Short Gold: Real yield pressure maintains bearish momentum
📉 SHORT
• AUD/USD: China growth concerns drive commodity currency weakness
• EUR/USD: ECB dovishness supports breakdown below 1.16
• Risk assets: Defensive rotation favors quality over growth

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: German inflation preliminary data, US initial jobless claims, and Fed speakers commentary remain key catalysts. Monitor 10Y Treasury yields and crude oil supply updates for directional cues.
